extraction.one_line_summary: CAVENDISH Mark won the Stage 3 bunch sprint from 300m out after Quick-Step Alpha Vinyl broke their 4-year Giro stage win drought
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step controlled final kilometer despite no Giro stage win in 4 years, suggesting renewed sprint train confidence.
Pascal Eenkorn attacked pre-categorized climb 12km from finish for KOM points, revealing GC teams allowed non-threatening moves late.
Cavendish launched at 300 meters, unusually early for modern sprint finishes, yet held off faster closers through positioning.
Alpecine-Fenix led with 3km remaining but faded by 1km, exposing their leadout depth compared to Quick-Step.

rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: Won at nearly 37 years old after 9-year Giro absence, suggesting form peaked specifically for this race block
rider_signals.DE MAAR Arnaud: positive: Second place without dedicated leadout indicates raw speed competitive with supported sprinters
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: neutral: Third place matches pre-race form expectations but couldn't match Cavendish's early jump timing
rider_signals.NIZZOLO Giacomo: negative: Alpecine-Fenix leadout dissolved before final kilometer, failed to deliver promised sprint support
rider_signals.EENKORN Pascal: positive: Attacked for KOM points despite GC pressure building, shows KOM jersey ambitions overriding team sprint duties
